section.title: Grades
section.p: General PCC Grading Guidelines are found at .

paragraphs.title: Video Lecture Notes (4%)
paragraphs.p: Taking notes during the lectures helps students understand and retain information. Each section has lecture notes that accompany YouTube videos. Print these lecture note outlines and fill them in as you watch the videos. (Or annotate a PDF digitally.) Post them to the appropriate place in D2L each week. The video lecture note files are available in D2L, from the Quick Reference navbar item, in Downloads.

paragraphs.title: Runestone Homework (24%)
paragraphs.p: You have online homework for each section using Runestone Academy. See for details. The due date for each assignment is Sunday after we covered it in class. Usually, more than one section will be due at the same time. You should start working on a homework assignment soon after the class that is associated with that section of the book. Always read the textbook section, watch the video lectures, and complete the video lecture notes before beginning the Runestone exercises.

paragraphs.title: Worksheets/Activities (24%)
paragraphs.p: Most class sessions there is a worksheet or activity to complete and turn in. You will work together and with me to complete these activities. You will get my feedback on your math writing skills. If you miss a class or don't complete something during our class time, you can still complete the asignment outside of class and turn it in using D2L.

paragraphs.title: Exams (11% + 11% + 11% + 15%)
paragraphs.p: There are chapter exams and a final exam. Each exam is proctored. Most students will take each exam during one of our class sessions. See for possible alternative arrangements.

paragraphs.title: Grading Options
paragraphs.p: Students are solely responsible for adding, dropping, or withdrawing from any class, as well as for selecting their desired grading option. Please review PCC Registration Policy for more information.

paragraphs.title: Letter Grades
paragraphs.p: Your letter grade will be assigned based on the weighted average of the items above.
